    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_migrate_column_data_default_value_for_null(
        entrypoint_argv, seeds_dir, tmp_path
    ):
        """Functionality: Default value is used when from_column is NULL."""
        migration_file = tmp_path / "migration.json"
        db_file = tmp_path / "app.db"
        shutil.copy(seeds_dir / "checkpoint_2_users.db", db_file)
    
        with sqlite3.connect(db_file) as conn:
            conn.execute("UPDATE users SET old_email = NULL WHERE id = 1")
            conn.commit()
    
        migration = {
            "version": 2,
            "description": "Migrate emails with default for NULL",
            "operations": [
                {
                    "type": "add_column",
                    "table": "users",
                    "column": {"name": "new_email", "type": "TEXT", "not_null": False},
                },
                {
                    "type": "migrate_column_data",
                    "table": "users",
                    "from_column": "old_email",
                    "to_column": "new_email",
                    "default_value": "'unknown'",
                },
            ],
        }
    
        migration_file.write_text(json.dumps(migration))
    
        result = run_command(
            entrypoint_argv, "migrate", str(migration_file), str(db_file)
        )
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, f"Command failed: {result.stderr}"
        events = parse_jsonl(result.stdout)
        assert events == [
            {"event": "operation_applied", "type": "add_column", "table": "users", "column": "new_email", "version": 2},
            {"event": "operation_applied", "type": "migrate_column_data", "table": "users", "from_column": "old_email", "to_column": "new_email", "version": 2},
            {"event": "migration_complete", "version": 2, "operations_count": 2},
        ]
    
        with sqlite3.connect(db_file) as conn:

                "SELECT id, old_email, new_email FROM users ORDER BY id"

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_transformations_applied_in_order(
        entrypoint_argv, seeds_dir, tmp_path
    ):
        """Functionality: Transformations run in the order listed."""
        migration_file = tmp_path / "migration.json"
        db_file = tmp_path / "app.db"
        shutil.copy(seeds_dir / "checkpoint_2_users.db", db_file)
    
        migration = {
            "version": 2,
            "description": "Uppercase first_name before full_name",
            "operations": [
                {
                    "type": "add_column",
                    "table": "users",
                    "column": {"name": "full_name", "type": "TEXT", "not_null": False},
                },
                {
                    "type": "transform_data",
                    "table": "users",
                    "transformations": [
                        {"column": "first_name", "expression": "upper(first_name)"},
                        {
                            "column": "full_name",
                            "expression": "first_name || ' ' || last_name",
                        },
                    ],
                },
            ],
        }
    
        migration_file.write_text(json.dumps(migration))
    
        result = run_command(
            entrypoint_argv, "migrate", str(migration_file), str(db_file)
        )
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, f"Command failed: {result.stderr}"
        events = parse_jsonl(result.stdout)
        assert events == [
            {"event": "operation_applied", "type": "add_column", "table": "users", "column": "full_name", "version": 2},
            {"event": "operation_applied", "type": "transform_data", "table": "users", "version": 2},
            {"event": "migration_complete", "version": 2, "operations_count": 2},
        ]
    
        with sqlite3.connect(db_file) as conn:
            row = conn.execute(
                "SELECT first_name, full_name FROM users WHERE id = 1"
            ).fetchone()

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_drop_foreign_key(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path):
        """Functionality: Drop a foreign key constraint."""
        migration_v1_file = tmp_path / "migration_v1.json"
        migration_v2_file = tmp_path / "migration_v2.json"
        db_file = tmp_path / "app.db"
    
        migration_v1 = {
            "version": 1,
            "description": "Create posts with foreign key",
            "operations": [
                {
                    "type": "create_table",
                    "table": "users",
                    "columns": [
                        {"name": "id", "type": "INTEGER", "primary_key": True},
                    ],
                },
                {
                    "type": "create_table",
                    "table": "posts",
                    "columns": [
                        {"name": "id", "type": "INTEGER", "primary_key": True},
                        {"name": "user_id", "type": "INTEGER", "not_null": True},
                    ],
                },
                {
                    "type": "add_foreign_key",
                    "table": "posts",
                    "name": "fk_posts_user_id",
                    "columns": ["user_id"],
                    "references": {"table": "users", "columns": ["id"]},
                    "on_delete": "CASCADE",
                },
            ],
        }
    
        migration_v2 = {
            "version": 2,
            "description": "Drop posts foreign key",
            "operations": [
                {
                    "type": "drop_foreign_key",
                    "table": "posts",
                    "name": "fk_posts_user_id",
                }
            ],
        }
    
        migration_v1_file.write_text(json.dumps(migration_v1))
        migration_v2_file.write_text(json.dumps(migration_v2))
    
        result = run_command(
            entrypoint_argv, "migrate", str(migration_v1_file), str(db_file)
        )
        assert result.returncode == 0, f"Setup failed: {result.stderr}"
    
        result = run_command(
            entrypoint_argv, "migrate", str(migration_v2_file), str(db_file)
        )
        assert result.returncode == 0, f"Command failed: {result.stderr}"
        events = parse_jsonl(result.stdout)
        assert events == [
            {
                "event": "operation_applied",
                "type": "drop_foreign_key",
                "table": "posts",
                "name": "fk_posts_user_id",
                "version": 2,
            },
            {"event": "migration_complete", "version": 2, "operations_count": 1},
        ]
    
        with sqlite3.connect(db_file) as conn:
            fks = conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_key_list(posts)").fetchall()

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', 'migration_tool.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_transactional_rollback_on0')

    @pytest.mark.error
    def test_transactional_rollback_on_failure(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path):
        """Error: Failed op should roll back prior operations."""
        migration_file = tmp_path / "migration.json"
        db_file = tmp_path / "app.db"
    
        with sqlite3.connect(db_file) as conn:
            conn.execute(
                "CREATE TABLE posts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER)"
            )
            conn.commit()
    
        migration = {
            "version": 1,
            "description": "Create index then fail",
            "operations": [
                {
                    "type": "create_index",
                    "name": "idx_posts_user_id",
                    "table": "posts",
                    "columns": ["user_id"],
                    "unique": False,
                },
                {
                    "type": "add_foreign_key",
                    "table": "posts",
                    "name": "fk_posts_user_id",
                    "columns": ["user_id"],
                    "references": {"table": "users", "columns": ["id"]},
                },
            ],
        }
    
        migration_file.write_text(json.dumps(migration))
    
        result = run_command(
            entrypoint_argv, "migrate", str(migration_file), str(db_file)
        )
    
        assert result.returncode == 1, "Expected command to fail with exit code 1"
    
        with sqlite3.connect(db_file) as conn:
            indexes = conn.execute("PRAGMA index_list(posts)").fetchall()
            try:
                applied = conn.execute(
                    "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM _migrations WHERE version = 1"
                ).fetchone()[0]
            except sqlite3.OperationalError:

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_explicit_rollback_operations_override_automatic_rollback(
        entrypoint_argv, tmp_path
    ):
        """Functionality: Explicit rollback ops take precedence over automatic ones."""
        migration_v1_file = tmp_path / "migration_v1.json"
        migration_v2_file = tmp_path / "migration_v2.json"
        db_file = tmp_path / "app.db"
    
        migration_v1 = {
            "version": 1,
            "description": "Create users table",
            "operations": [
                {
                    "type": "create_table",
                    "table": "users",
                    "columns": [
                        {"name": "id", "type": "INTEGER", "primary_key": True},
                        {"name": "email", "type": "TEXT"},
                    ],
                }
            ],
        }
    
        migration_v2 = {
            "version": 2,
            "description": "Add new_email column with explicit rollback",
            "operations": [
                {
                    "type": "add_column",
                    "table": "users",
                    "column": {"name": "new_email", "type": "TEXT"},
                },
                {
                    "type": "migrate_column_data",
                    "table": "users",
                    "from_column": "email",
                    "to_column": "new_email",
                }
            ],
            "rollback_operations": [
                {
                    "type": "migrate_column_data",
                    "table": "users",
                    "from_column": "new_email",
                    "to_column": "email",
                }
            ],
        }
    
        migration_v1_file.write_text(json.dumps(migration_v1))
        migration_v2_file.write_text(json.dumps(migration_v2))

    @pytest.mark.error
    def test_rollback_transactional_on_failure(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path):
        """Error: Failed rollback should not partially apply changes."""
        migration_file = tmp_path / "migration.json"
        db_file = tmp_path / "app.db"
    
        migration = {
            "version": 1,
            "description": "Create parents with index",
            "operations": [
                {
                    "type": "create_table",
                    "table": "parents",
                    "columns": [
                        {"name": "id", "type": "INTEGER", "primary_key": True},
                    ],
                },
                {
                    "type": "create_index",
                    "name": "idx_parents_id",
                    "table": "parents",
                    "columns": ["id"],
                    "unique": False,
                },
            ],
        }
    
        migration_file.write_text(json.dumps(migration))
    
        result = run_command(entrypoint_argv, "migrate", str(migration_file), str(db_file))
        assert result.returncode == 0, f"Setup failed: {result.stderr}"
    
        with sqlite3.connect(db_file) as conn:
            conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
            conn.execute(
                "CREATE TABLE children (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, parent_id INTEGER, "
                "FOREIGN KEY(parent_id) REFERENCES parents(id))"
            )
            conn.execute("INSERT INTO parents (id) VALUES (?)", (1,))
            conn.execute(
                "INSERT INTO children (id, parent_id) VALUES (?, ?)", (1, 1)
            )
            conn.commit()
    
        result = run_command(entrypoint_argv, "rollback", str(db_file))
    
        assert result.returncode == 1, "Expected command to fail with exit code 1"
    
        with sqlite3.connect(db_file) as conn:
            indexes = conn.execute("PRAGMA index_list(parents)").fetchall()
            applied = conn.execute(
                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM _migrations WHERE version = 1"
            ).fetchone()[0]

    def test_validate_command(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path):
        """Core: validate command checks migration file with --migrations-dir."""
        migrations_dir = tmp_path / "migrations"
        migrations_dir.mkdir()
    
        # Migration v1: Create users table
        (migrations_dir / "migration_v1.json").write_text(
            json.dumps(
                {
                    "version": 1,
                    "description": "Create users table",
                    "operations": [
                        {
                            "type": "create_table",
                            "table": "users",
                            "columns": [
                                {
                                    "name": "id",
                                    "type": "INTEGER",
                                    "primary_key": True,
                                    "auto_increment": True,
                                },
                                {
                                    "name": "email",

                                },

        )

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', 'migration_tool.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_migrate_skips_already_app0')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_migrate_skips_already_applied_dependencies(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path):
        """Functionality: Single-target migrate skips already-applied ancestors."""
        db_file = tmp_path / "app.db"
        migrations_dir = tmp_path / "migrations"
        migrations_dir.mkdir()
    
        migration_v1 = {
            "version": 1,
            "description": "Create users table",
            "operations": [
                {
                    "type": "create_table",
                    "table": "users",
                    "columns": [
                        {"name": "id", "type": "INTEGER", "primary_key": True}
                    ],
                }
            ],
        }
        migration_v2 = {
            "version": 2,
            "description": "Add age column",
            "depends_on": [1],
            "operations": [
                {
                    "type": "add_column",
                    "table": "users",
                    "column": {"name": "age", "type": "INTEGER"},
                }
            ],
        }
        migration_v3 = {
            "version": 3,
            "description": "Add name column",
            "depends_on": [2],
            "operations": [
                {
                    "type": "add_column",
                    "table": "users",
                    "column": {"name": "name", "type": "TEXT"},
                }
            ],
        }
    
        (migrations_dir / "migration_v1.json").write_text(json.dumps(migration_v1))
        (migrations_dir / "migration_v2.json").write_text(json.dumps(migration_v2))
        (migrations_dir / "migration_v3.json").write_text(json.dumps(migration_v3))
    
        migration_v1_file = tmp_path / "migration_v1.json"
        migration_v1_file.write_text(json.dumps(migration_v1))
        migration_v3_file = tmp_path / "migration_v3.json"
        migration_v3_file.write_text(json.dumps(migration_v3))
    
        result = run_command(
            entrypoint_argv, "migrate", str(migration_v1_file), str(db_file)
        )
        assert result.returncode == 0, f"Setup failed: {result.stderr}"
    
        result = run_command(
            entrypoint_argv,
            "migrate",
            str(migration_v3_file),
            str(db_file),
            "--migrations-dir",
            str(migrations_dir) + "/",
        )
